title: penstemon carnosus
intro: Penstemon carnosus, also known as Beard-tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family that is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Penstemon carnosus are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Penstemon carnosus can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by using the plant in a salve or tincture to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
